text: Being here, I feel a subtle curiosity, mingled with a tinge of self-consciousness. The atmosphere is a controlled chaos: people’s voices murmur and echo, expectations drift around the room like invisible currents. There is a sense of anticipation, as if something meaningful might occur, yet also the inescapable awareness of my own presence among strangers and the everyday machinery of the world—fire hoses on walls, exposed pipes above. The boundaries between observer and participant seem blurred.
